description: Letter fragment in Judaeo-Arabic addressed to Eliyyahu the Judge. The sender complains that people are “opposing me by submitting responsa to the head of the Jews" (though כלפוה might be kallafa "tasked" rather than khālafa "opposed," in which case he is being tasked by the people to forward their legal queries to sayyidnā al-rayyis). He asks Eliyya to forward the legal queries and/or responsa from where they have been deposited with "a certain perfumer." Greetings to Sitt Rayḥān (Eliyyahu's wife) and his sons Abu l-Barakāt (Shelomo) and Abū Zikrī. (Information in part from Oded Zinger's PhD, p. 210)
